REMOVED FROM MASTER COURT CALENDAR SET FOR DEC-14-2015 - CASE SETTLED AT THE NOV-20-2015 MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF IXC HOLDINGS, INC. MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT. A R G U E D. DEFENSE COUNSEL INFORMS THE COURT THAT THEY ARE IN PROCESS OF FILING A SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY, AND REQUESTS A CONTINUANCE TO FILE A FORMAL OPPOSITION. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 20, 2017, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, ON THE CONDITION THAT DEFENDANT WTI COMMUNICATIONS, INC., REMAINS CURRENT WITH THEIR PAYMENT OBLIGATIONS. AN OPPOSITION SHALL BE FILED BY MARCH 30, 2017; AND THE REPLY SHALL BE FILED BY APRIL 10, 2017. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF IXC HOLDINGS, INC.'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT IS DENIED ON THE CONDITION THAT ON OR BEFORE MAY 5, 2017 DEFENDANT WTI COMMUNICATIONS, INC. PAY $6,850 PLUS ALL MONTHLY PAYMENTS DUE UNDER THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AS OF MAY 1, 2017 THAT HAD NOT YET BEEN PAID. THE $6,850 INCLUDES THE UNPAID $5,000 BALLOON PAYMENT, INTEREST THEREON, AND FEES AND COSTS FOR THE FILING OF THIS MOTION. THE FAILURE TO TIMELY CURE THE DEFAULT ON THE BALLOON PAYMENT APPEARS TO HAVE BEEN THE RESULT OF A MISTAKE. EVEN IF IT WAS A BREACH MATERIAL, WTI PROVIDES A REASONABLE EXCUSE. IN ALL EVENTS, IXC IS NOT ENTITLED TO THE REQUESTED JUDGMENT FOR $227,209.37 BECAUSE THE "EARLY TERMINATION CHARGES" ARE AN ILLEGAL PENALTY PER CIVIL CODE 1671 AND GREENTREE FINANCIAL GROUP, INC. V. EXECUTE SPORTS, INC. (2008) 163 CAL.APP.4TH 495. IF WTI DOES NOT MAKE THE REQUIRED PAYMENTS BY MAY 5, IXC MAY MAKE AN EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR A JUDGMENT BASED ON A DECLARATION ITEMIZING THE AMOUNT THEN DUE NOT INCLUDING ANY "EARLY TERMINATION CHARGES." PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO SEND BY WAY OF E-MAIL. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 =302/HK

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-24-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-20-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PAL A. LENGYEL-LEAHU, TEL. NO. 1-714-497-6813 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff IXC Holdings, Inc.'s motion for entry of judgment is denied on the condition that on or before May 5, 2107 defendant WTI Communications, Inc. pay $6,850 plus all monthly payments due under the settlement agreement as of May 1, 2017 that had not yet been paid. The $6,850 includes the unpaid $5,000 balloon payment, interest thereon, and fees and costs for the filing of this motion. The failure to timely cure the default on the balloon payment appears to have been the result of a mistake. Even if it was a breach material, WTI provides a reasonable excuse. In all events, IXC is not entitled to the requested judgment for $227,209.37 because the "early termination charges" are an illegal penalty per Civil Code 1671 and Greentree Financial Group, Inc. v. Execute Sports, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 495. If WTI does not make the required payments by May 5, IXC may make an ex parte application for a judgment based on a declaration itemizing the amount then due not including any "early termination charges." Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to send by way of e-mail. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-07-2017 Continued To Apr-20-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ronald K. Losch, of Losch & Ehrlich, Tel. 415.956.8400, for plaintiffs Telekenex Acquisition Corporation;
Pal A. Lengyel-Leahu, Law Offices of Pal Lengyel-Leahu, Tel. 714.497.6813, for defendants WTI Communications, Inc.

Ruling: A R G U E D. Defense counsel informs the Court that they are in process of filing a substitution of attorney, and requests a continuance to file a formal opposition. PLAINTIFF IXC HOLDINGS, INC., Motion For Entry Of Judgment is continued to April 20, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, on the condition that defendant WTI Communications, Inc., remains current with their payment obligations. An Opposition shall be filed by March 30, 2017; and the Reply shall be filed by April 10, 2017. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
